title: Improve proposal execution UX: run-in-chat default, visibility, and command reliability
summary: Make approved proposals execute in the same chat by default, expose clear proposal↔task visibility, and enforce reliable validation command execution with real exit-code checks.
details:
This proposal improves trust and debuggability around approved proposal execution.

Scope:
1) Default execution routing:
- When a proposal is approved from an active chat, execution should default to that same session/thread unless explicitly overridden.
- Preserve backward-compatible options for isolated/background execution.

2) Proposal-task observability:
- Persist and display a first-class link between proposal ID and task ID.
- Surface execution state in both proposal panel and chat timeline (queued/running/paused/failed/complete).
- Add quick actions: open task, view logs, resume, rerun.

3) Better status messaging:
- Show explicit reason text when waiting/paused (e.g., awaiting tool, awaiting approval, tool timeout, validation failed).
- Remove ambiguity where users see terminal logs but no navigable task object.

4) Command reliability hardening:
- Disallow detached command patterns for validations (e.g., `start cmd /k ...`) in proposal executors.
- Run build/test in a blocking process and gate completion on exit code.
- Mark step complete only after command exits successfully; otherwise fail with actionable error output.

Acceptance criteria:
- Approve-in-chat runs in-chat by default.
- Every approved proposal has a visible task record with a stable cross-link.
- Build/test steps cannot falsely report success while still running detached.
- Failed validation clearly blocks final completion and reports stderr/stdout context.

affectedFiles.description: Route approved proposals to current chat session by default; preserve explicit override paths.
Persist proposal-to-task linkage metadata and expose retrieval helpers.
Render proposal execution state, reasons, and quick navigation to linked task/logs.
Enforce blocking command execution and exit-code-gated step completion for validation commands.
Add guardrails/warnings for detached shell launches in validation contexts.

estimatedImpact: Higher user trust and debuggability; eliminates false-positive completion from detached commands; reduces confusion around where approved work runs.
executorPrompt: Implement the approved proposal exactly as specified: default approved proposals to run in the originating chat session unless overridden, add durable proposal↔task linkage and status reason visibility in UI, and harden validation command execution to blocking/exit-code-gated behavior (no detached cmd /k patterns). Update the listed files, run build/tests, and report final diffs and validation output.
